title: Running Two Models Together
concept: Speculative decoding speeds up inference by running a small, fast 'draft' model and a large, slow 'target' model together.

exercises.prompt: How many models are run together in speculative decoding?
exercises.explain: Speculative decoding uses two models: a small 'draft' model and a large 'target' model.

cards.title: Speeding Up Big AIs
cards.body: Have you ever wished giant AI models could talk faster? Waiting for a massive AI to think of every single word can be slow. But what if we gave the giant AI a tiny, speedy helper to write drafts first?

cards.title: What is Speculative Decoding?
cards.body: Speculative decoding is a trick where a small, super-fast 'draft' model guesses the next few words first. Then, a large, highly accurate 'target' model checks those guesses. If they are correct, we keep them! This makes text generation much faster.

cards.title: The Chef and the Apprentice
cards.body: Imagine a master chef and an apprentice. The apprentice quickly chops the vegetables (the draft model), and the master chef glances at them. If they look good, the master uses them instantly. If not, the master recuts them. Because the apprentice does most of the fast prep, the master chef finishes the dish much faster!

cards.title: Drafting in Action
cards.body: When you start typing 'The sky is...', the tiny draft model might quickly predict 'blue and beautiful'. The big target model checks this guess. If it agrees, all three words are created in the blink of an eye, instead of waiting for the slow model to write them one by one.

cards.title: Double the Work?
cards.body: People think that using two models instead of one must take twice as much time. But because the small model is lightning-fast and the big model only has to verify the work rather than think from scratch, the whole process actually becomes much quicker!

cards.title: One Model vs. Two Together
cards.body: Compare how standard generation works versus speculative decoding with two models working together.

left.text: One large, slow model generates every single word one by one.

right.text: A small model drafts words quickly, and the large model approves them in batches.

exercises.prompt: Why do we use speculative decoding?

options.text: To speed up text generation in large models
To make the AI write in different languages
To train models using less training data
To replace the large model completely

exercises.explain: Speculative decoding is designed to speed up the process of generating text by using a smaller model to help the larger one.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Speculative decoding keeps the high quality of the large model while speeding things up.
exercises.explain: Even though the draft model makes guesses, the final output must be approved by the target model, ensuring it remains highly accurate.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of speculative decoding:
exercises.sentence: Speculative decoding uses a fast [blank] model to guess words, which are then verified by a slower target model.
exercises.explain: The draft model makes quick guesses, while the target model verifies them to ensure accuracy.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is using speculative decoding?

options.text: System A: A tiny assistant model drafts words, and a large model approves them.
System B: A single massive model writes every single word on its own.
System C: A model randomly picks words from a dictionary.

exercises.explain: System A represents speculative decoding because it uses a small model to assist a larger target model. System B is just a standard single-model system.

exercises.prompt: You want to speed up your AI chatbot using speculative decoding. How do the two models work together to achieve this?

options.text: The draft model guesses words quickly, and the target model checks them in one go.
Both models generate different stories and combine them.
The target model does all the work while the draft model sleeps.
It runs two large models at the exact same time to double the memory.

exercises.explain: Speculative decoding pairs a fast draft model with a slow target model. The draft model guesses multiple words ahead, which the target model verifies all at once, speeding up the overall process.
